programmes.show_description: Get your fix of all the best new releases in one handy playlist.
James Blake talks new records and a year of change, and Kim Gordon chats to Mary Anne Hobbs. Plus, Matt Everitt hosts packages from The Sophs and Art School Girlfriend.
6 Music takes you through the early hours playing back-to-back music.
6 Music goes back to the 90s!
6 Music goes back to the 00s!
Chris takes you through 30 minutes of remixed, re-versioned and re-imagined tracks to help you chill. With Nightmares on Wax, Massive Attack, Unknown Mortal Orchestra and more.
Chris with the early morning breakfast show.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Radio 1's Greg James takes us to the Desert Island Disco.
Craig spins some joyous tunes to get your weekend started, before giving YOU the chance to pick the last song of the show!
It's Friday, so Huw is out to prove that any song sounds better when it's played on 6 Music by Huw Stephens - so let us know what you want to hear.
Huw Stephens gets set for the 6 Music Festival 2026 with another mix of ultimate indie bangers.

programmes.show_name: The People's Party with Femi Koleoso
programmes.show_description: The British-Nigerian singer-songwriter gives you a sneak peek of her Friday nights ahead of her long-awaited sophomore album release, "Good Intentions".
Nathan plays indie classics from Two Door Cinema Club and Los Campesinos!
Nath plays an hour of Slacker Indie from Mac DeMarco, Courtney Barnett and Pavement!

programmes.show_name: The 6 Mix with Mary Anne Hobbs
programmes.show_description: The Cure frontman Robert Smith takes over the 6 Mix ahead of his 'Cureation' of this year's Teenage Cancer Trust concerts at the at the Royal Albert Hall featuring MBV and Mogwai.
Celebrating the eclectic remix catalogue of Kieran Hebden, better known as Four Tet.
6 Music takes you to the dark side with the finest goth, electronic and dark pop tracks around, featuring This Mortal Coil, Dot Allison, John Glacier, Barry Can't Swim and Wardruna.
Cillian handpicks music by Portishead, Aretha Franklin, A Tribe Called Quest, David Bowie, Low and Arthur Russell.
Icelandic post rock and ambient band Sigur Rós select two hours of absorbing ambient music to help you find your focus.
Afrodeutsche brings the mellow weekend flow you need to hit that deeply relaxing vibe.
Mark Radcliffe presents a feast of great music and singer-songwriter Tori Amos shares her 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about her new album.
Deb sets the weekend vibe with a magical multigenre selection from house to dub to hip-hop to rock to soul and beyond - including music from Sault, Jai Paul, Hole and Stevie Wonder.
Jamz gets your weekend party started with an uplifting array of alternative RnB, broken beat, global club sounds and leftfield electronica.
Joining the musical dots, soul, hip-hop, house, Afro, Latin, electronica, jazz and beyond.

programmes.show_name: The Craig Charles Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: The Trunk of Funk comes from The Bandulus, a soul and reggae outfit led by producer and musician Jeremy Peña.
Don's Saturday night selections cross time, space and genre. This week, he's got numbers by Primal Scream, Dub Pistols, Hollie Cook, Tinlicker and Zion Train.
SHERELLE welcomes rising pop star Natanya onto the show to chat about her musical beginnings, her affinity with jazz music, and her latest project 'Feline's Return Act II'.
